name: Fertilizer + Iron
talk: Fescue grows actively in cool weather. This feeds the lawn coming out of winter with slow-release nitrogen and iron for deep green color. We'll spot-treat any broadleaf weeds we see.
yard: Actively growing in above-freezing temps. Greener, thicker, healthier.

name: Pre-Emergent + Post-Emergent + Fertilizer
talk: Active growing season — we're feeding the lawn, putting down a pre-emergent barrier for spring/summer weeds, and killing anything that's already growing. Three things in one visit.
yard: Actively growing. Weed prevention for spring/summer. Thicker turf.

name: Pre-Emergent + Post-Emergent + Fertilizer
talk: Reinforcing the pre-emergent with a second chemistry before summer (prevents resistance), continued nutrition, and broadleaf weed control.
yard: Active growth continues. Spring/summer weed barrier reinforced.

name: Soil Health + Post-Emergent
talk: Fescue slows down in summer heat — this is its stressful period. Earth Max supports soil biology and stress tolerance. We're still managing weeds to reduce competition for the grass.
yard: Slowing growth. Nutrition to sustain through summer heat stress.

name: Fertilizer + Lime (Post-Seeding)
talk: After overseeding, this phosphorus-heavy fertilizer with lime feeds new seed and adjusts soil pH. Phosphorus promotes root establishment — exactly what baby grass needs.
yard: New grass is planted. Nutrition promotes new growth and establishment.

name: Fertilizer + Iron
talk: Fall is fescue's prime growing season. Another round of slow-release nutrition with iron for thickness and color. Spot-treating weeds with Tenacity, which is excellent for fall fescue.
yard: Actively growing. Prime season for thickening and recovery.

name: Post-Emergent + Fertilizer
talk: Growth is slowing — we're cleaning up winter broadleaf weeds and giving a last nutrition push. Highest kelp rate of the year to carry the lawn through winter.
yard: Slowing growth. Weed control for winter/spring weeds applied.
